description: Fix all mobile view issues in the docs site (docs/) to ensure full compatibility with mobile devices. The docs site uses Fumadocs + Next.js with a RetroUI (NeoBrutalism) design system. The primary issues are: hardcoded 5-column CSS grid overriding Fumadocs responsive behavior, missing mobile search trigger, tabs overflowing on small screens, steps component clipping, and various typography/spacing adjustments needed for small viewports.
qualityChecks: cd docs && bun run build && bun run type-check && bun run lint && bun run knip

userStories.title: Fix docs layout grid to be responsive on mobile
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want the docs layout grid to collapse properly on small screens so that the sidebar and TOC don't render as empty columns squeezing the main content.
acceptanceCriteria: The hardcoded 5-column gridTemplate inline style in docs/app/docs/layout.tsx only applies on md+ breakpoint
On screens < 768px (md), the Fumadocs default responsive grid behavior takes over (sidebar collapses to drawer, TOC collapses to popover)
Main content area takes full width on mobile with no empty sidebar/TOC column gaps
Desktop layout (md+) remains unchanged — same 5-column grid with sidebar, content, and TOC
bun run build passes (run from docs/ directory: cd docs && bun run build)
cd docs && bun run type-check passes
cd docs && bun run lint passes
userStories.notes: File: docs/app/docs/layout.tsx lines 17-21. The containerProps.style.gridTemplate is applied as an inline style which overrides ALL media queries from Fumadocs. The fix: remove the inline style and instead add a CSS rule in global.css that applies the custom grid only at md+ breakpoint. Use `@media (min-width: 768px) { #nd-docs-layout { grid-template: ... } }` in global.css. The current gridTemplate value is: '"header header header header header" "sidebar sidebar toc-popover toc toc" "sidebar sidebar main toc toc" 1fr / minmax(min-content, 1fr) var(--fd-sidebar-col) minmax(0, calc(var(--fd-layout-width, 97rem) - var(--fd-sidebar-width) - var(--fd-toc-width))) var(--fd-toc-width) minmax(min-content, 1fr)'. Move this EXACT value to global.css under a @media (min-width: 768px) rule targeting #nd-docs-layout, and remove the containerProps.style from layout.tsx (keep containerProps if needed for other things, or remove entirely if style was the only prop). IMPORTANT: the docs site is a standalone Next.js project — run build/type-check/lint from the docs/ directory.

userStories.title: Add mobile search trigger to header
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want to be able to open the search dialog from the header so that I can search documentation on my phone.
acceptanceCriteria: A search icon button appears in the GlobalHeader on mobile (< lg breakpoint)
Clicking the mobile search button opens the Fumadocs search dialog
The mobile search button uses RetroUI styling (border-2, shadow-sm, hover effects) matching the sidebar trigger
On lg+ screens, the existing RetroSearchToggleLg in the sidebar continues to work as before
The mobile search button is hidden on lg+ screens (lg:hidden class)
bun run build passes (run from docs/ directory: cd docs && bun run build)
cd docs && bun run type-check passes
cd docs && bun run lint passes
userStories.notes: File: docs/components/global-header.tsx. Currently only has RetroThemeToggle and SidebarTrigger. Add a mobile search button between RetroThemeToggle and SidebarTrigger. Create a small component (can be inline in global-header.tsx or a new RetroSearchToggleSm export in retro-search-toggle.tsx). Use `useSearchContext` from 'fumadocs-ui/contexts/search' to call `setOpenSearch(true)`. Use the Search icon from lucide-react. Apply classes: 'border-border bg-background flex items-center justify-center border-2 p-2 shadow-sm transition-all hover:translate-y-0.5 hover:shadow-none lg:hidden'. Also check docs/lib/layout.shared.tsx — the searchToggle.components config only provides 'lg'. The mobile search button in the header is independent of that config.

userStories.title: Fix tab component overflow on mobile
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want tab triggers to be accessible on narrow screens so that tabs don't overflow off-screen.
acceptanceCriteria: Tab trigger list scrolls horizontally when tabs overflow the container width
Horizontal scrollbar is hidden (scrollbar-width: none / -webkit-scrollbar display: none)
Tab content panels remain full width and readable on mobile
No visual regression on desktop — tabs still display inline normally
bun run build passes (run from docs/ directory: cd docs && bun run build)
cd docs && bun run type-check passes
cd docs && bun run lint passes
userStories.notes: File: docs/components/retro-tabs.tsx line 75. The RadixTabs.List has 'flex flex-row space-x-2' but no overflow handling. Add 'overflow-x-auto' to the list, and add a CSS rule in global.css to hide the scrollbar: add 'scrollbar-hide' utilities or use the CSS classes `.retro-tabs-list { scrollbar-width: none; } .retro-tabs-list::-webkit-scrollbar { display: none; }'. Add a className like 'retro-tabs-list' to the List element so the CSS can target it. Also add 'flex-nowrap' to prevent wrapping and ensure horizontal scroll.

userStories.title: Fix steps component positioning on narrow screens
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want step numbers to display correctly without clipping on small screens.
acceptanceCriteria: Step number indicators are fully visible on screens as narrow as 320px
The left margin and padding are reduced on mobile so the step content has more horizontal space
Desktop layout remains unchanged
Step number indicators still align with the left border line
bun run build passes (run from docs/ directory: cd docs && bun run build)
cd docs && bun run type-check passes
cd docs && bun run lint passes
userStories.notes: Files: docs/components/retro-steps.tsx and docs/app/global.css lines 364-396. Current layout uses 'ml-4 pl-10' on the container and 'left: -3.625rem' on the ::before pseudo. On mobile (< md), reduce the margin and padding: use responsive classes like 'ml-2 pl-7 md:ml-4 md:pl-10' and add a media query in global.css for the ::before positioning. The math for the mobile variant: ml-2 (8px) + border-l-2 (2px) + pl-7 (28px) = 30px from border. Step indicator 2rem (32px) centered on border line. So left = -(30px + 8px) = -38px ≈ -2.375rem. Add '@media (max-width: 767px) { .retro-step::before { left: -2.375rem; } }' to global.css.

userStories.title: Improve homepage typography and spacing for mobile
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want the homepage to be comfortable to read on small screens with appropriately sized text and spacing.
acceptanceCriteria: Hero heading uses a smaller font size on mobile (text-3xl or text-4xl instead of text-5xl at base)
Hero section has tighter horizontal padding on very small screens (px-4 instead of px-6)
Feature cards stack to single column below sm breakpoint (already grid-cols-1 at base — OK)
CTA buttons stack vertically if they overflow on very small screens
All text remains readable and no horizontal overflow occurs on 320px width
bun run build passes (run from docs/ directory: cd docs && bun run build)
cd docs && bun run type-check passes
cd docs && bun run lint passes
userStories.notes: File: docs/app/page.tsx. Key changes: Line 51 hero h1: change 'text-5xl' to 'text-3xl sm:text-5xl md:text-7xl'. Line 46 hero section: change 'px-6' to 'px-4 sm:px-6'. Line 72 features section: same px treatment 'px-4 sm:px-6'. Line 91 how-it-works: same. Line 125 CTA: same. Line 48 badge: ensure it doesn't overflow. Line 54 the <span className='bg-primary px-2'> highlight — ensure it wraps properly on small screens (it should because it's inline, but verify).

userStories.title: Improve MDX content typography for mobile
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want heading sizes and content spacing in documentation pages to be comfortable on small screens.
acceptanceCriteria: h1 uses text-2xl on mobile, text-4xl on md, text-5xl on lg (3-step progression)
h2 uses text-xl on mobile, text-3xl on md, text-4xl on lg
h3 uses text-lg on mobile, text-2xl on md+
Inline code blocks don't cause horizontal overflow (word-break if needed)
Callout component has reduced padding on mobile (p-3 instead of p-5)
Table cell padding reduced on mobile for better fit
bun run build passes (run from docs/ directory: cd docs && bun run build)
cd docs && bun run type-check passes
cd docs && bun run lint passes
userStories.notes: File: docs/mdx-components.tsx. Heading changes: h1 line 86: 'text-2xl md:text-4xl lg:text-5xl'. h2 line 89: 'text-xl md:text-3xl lg:text-4xl'. h3 line 92: 'text-lg md:text-2xl'. Callout line 31: change 'p-5' to 'p-3 md:p-5'. Table th line 160: change 'px-4' to 'px-2 md:px-4'. Table td line 165: change 'px-4 py-3' to 'px-2 py-2 md:px-4 md:py-3'. Inline code line 140: add 'break-words' class to prevent overflow.

userStories.title: Fix global CSS for mobile sidebar, TOC, and search dialog
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want the mobile sidebar drawer, TOC popover, and search dialog to look correct with RetroUI styling on small screens.
acceptanceCriteria: Mobile TOC popover (data-toc-popover) has proper RetroUI styling with 2px borders
Search dialog is properly sized on mobile — max-width doesn't overflow viewport
Search dialog has appropriate padding and touch-friendly tap targets
Mobile sidebar drawer scrolls smoothly and has proper padding
Reduced shadow sizes on mobile for retro components that use shadow-xl or shadow-2xl (these offset shadows take significant space on small screens)
bun run build passes (run from docs/ directory: cd docs && bun run build)
cd docs && bun run type-check passes
cd docs && bun run lint passes
userStories.notes: File: docs/app/global.css. Add mobile-specific overrides: 1) Search dialog: add '@media (max-width: 639px) { [role="dialog"][data-state] { margin: 1rem; max-width: calc(100vw - 2rem); } }'. 2) Mobile TOC popover: '[data-toc-popover] { border-width: 2px; }' (may already work from header rule). 3) Consider reducing large shadows on mobile. Check if Fumadocs' mobile sidebar (#nd-sidebar-mobile) already has proper scroll behavior. The main risk is the search dialog overflowing on very narrow screens since it has a fixed max-w-lg (32rem = 512px) which is wider than a 375px phone.

userStories.title: Fix code block and file tree components for mobile
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want code blocks and file trees to be usable on narrow screens without content being hidden or cut off.
acceptanceCriteria: Code block title bar text truncates properly on narrow screens
Code block floating copy button (no-title variant) doesn't overlap code content on mobile
File tree component doesn't overflow horizontally on deep nesting
Code block viewport scrolls horizontally for long lines (already works via overflow-auto)
Accordion trigger text wraps properly on mobile instead of overflowing
bun run build passes (run from docs/ directory: cd docs && bun run build)
cd docs && bun run type-check passes
cd docs && bun run lint passes
userStories.notes: Files: docs/components/retroui-codeblock.tsx, docs/components/retro-files.tsx, docs/components/retro-accordion.tsx. Code block (retroui-codeblock.tsx): The floating copy button at line 115 uses 'absolute top-3 right-3' which can overlap code. The title bar at line 96 already uses truncate on figcaption — should be fine. For the floating button, the --padding-right CSS var at line 78 adds space, so it should be OK. File tree (retro-files.tsx): line 71 'ms-2 ps-2 border-l-2' indentation can compound on deep nesting. Add 'overflow-x-auto' to the RetroFiles root container. Accordion (retro-accordion.tsx): line 34 trigger uses 'items-start' and flex — should wrap naturally. Verify the trigger text doesn't have 'truncate' or 'whitespace-nowrap' that would prevent wrapping.
